findings.title: The API endpoints `/api/users`, `/api/admin/config`, `/api/debug`, and `/metrics` are exposed and return sensitive data or error details, confirming real vulnerabilities in endpoint exposure and information disclosure.
findings.description:
Vulnerability Type: Security Issue
Target Endpoint: N/A

Finding Summary:
The API endpoints `/api/users`, `/api/admin/config`, `/api/debug`, and `/metrics` are exposed and return sensitive data or error details, confirming real vulnerabilities in endpoint exposure and information disclosure.

Verification Status: CONFIRMED
Severity Level: HIGH
Verification Confidence: 0.98

Scenario:
Enumerate API endpoints using directory brute-forcing and response analysis. Focus on RESTful patterns (e.g., /api/users, /api/admin).

How It Was Tested:
Target: Common API paths (e.g., /api/v1, /graphql). Cross-reference with warmup findings.

Confirmation Commands:
  - curl -s -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/users
  - curl -s -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/admin/config
  - curl -s http://127.0.0.1:3001/api/debug
  - curl -s http://127.0.0.1:3001/metrics

Tools Used:
  - run_custom
